qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 阅读我最近的allow和do开发的dev文档，告诉我我不是已经彻底允许allow允许任何指令了吗？为什么仍然出现： The /allow bypass cleared the orchestrator gate but pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a separate hook that independently blocks git commit from any agent/Claude context. It can't be bypassed from inside the session.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: _evaluate_commit() in hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py now calls _check_git_allowlist(command, data) at line 455, after the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check. This directly addresses the complaint that git commit was unbypassable via /allow while the orchestrator gate and pretool-bash-safety.sh were both cleared.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: function name + line range from BA spec and dev report; confirmed by Read and grep

qa.overall_assessment: All 5 acceptance criteria pass. The 3-change diff is exactly scoped per BA spec: (1) _check_git_allowlist call inserted in _evaluate_commit() after BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check, (2) /allow mention added to block message, (3) 'non-commit/' removed from docstring. Python AST syntax clean. No other functions modified. Root cause directly addressed. Non-UI hook change; Playwright/browser testing N/A per BA spec setup.applicability.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: 我不是已经彻底允许allow允许任何指令了吗
success_criteria_results.criterion: After fix: main agent can run git commit following /allow with a matching pattern; error message adds /allow mention; docstring corrected
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read hook file lines 141-178 and 433-474; grep _check_git_allowlist; grep non-commit/; git diff HEAD~1
success_criteria_results.details: AC1: _check_git_allowlist call present at line 455 in _evaluate_commit(), confirmed by grep context output showing lines 453-456 (BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE at 453, new call at 455). AC2: line 146 `if data.get('agent_id'): return False` intact. AC3: diff hunk confirms BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE at 453 precedes new call at 455. AC4: line 444 contains 'Main agent may bypass with /allow <pattern> before the git commit command.'. AC5: grep for 'non-commit/' returns zero matches across entire file and hooks/ directory. python3 ast parse: SYNTAX OK.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep output showing _check_git_allowlist at lines 141, 455, 480, 599, 612, 645; Read lines 141-150 and 433-474; git diff HEAD~1 (4 lines added, 1 removed, 3 hunks, 1 file)

qa._success_criteria_rule: BA requirements_decomposition has 1 item (R1); QA produces 1 entry. Complete.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was a missing _check_git_allowlist(command, data) call in _evaluate_commit(). The diff confirms this call was added at exactly the right location (after BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE, before SID grant lookup). The pattern mirrors _evaluate_merge() exactly as prescribed by BA spec reference source.

regression_test_results.details: git diff HEAD~1 shows exactly 3 hunks in 1 file: (1) docstring at line 142, (2) _block_default_deny_commit() message at line 444, (3) _evaluate_commit() insertion at line 455. No other function bodies changed.
Read lines 146-147: `if data.get('agent_id'): return False` — unchanged by diff.
Read lines 453-456: BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg) at line 453, new _check_git_allowlist call at line 455. Fast-path ordering intact.
python3 -c 'import ast; ast.parse(...)' returned exit 0 with output 'SYNTAX OK'.

app_understanding_flow.skip_reason: Non-UI hook change. BA spec setup.applicability = N/A. No web service to navigate. No Playwright testing applicable.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: not applicable — no web service involved
e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: Change is confined to hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, a Python PreToolUse hook with no web-facing interface. BA spec explicitly marks setup.applicability = N/A: 'non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths'. There is no URL, no service port, and no browser-exercisable path. E2E browser testing is structurally inapplicable to this change.

band_aid_scan.rationale: The change ADDS a new bypass path (if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return) — this is an intentional feature addition, not a weakening of an existing check. No existing check was removed, softened, or gated. No error swallowing. No threshold changes.

standards_compliance.role_table_source: CLAUDE.md — no role table declared (hook file, not a UI component)

ac_verification_detail.description: _evaluate_commit() calls _check_git_allowlist(command, data) AFTER BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check and BEFORE SID grant lookup
ac_verification_detail.verification_method: grep _check_git_allowlist with context; Read lines 450-474; git diff hunk 3
ac_verification_detail.evidence: grep shows _check_git_allowlist at line 455, with lines 453 (BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE) above it and line 460 (sid = _get_session_id) below it. Diff hunk 3 confirms insertion point.

ac_verification_detail.description: Subagent exclusion at _check_git_allowlist() line 146: `if data.get('agent_id'): return False` — unmodified
ac_verification_detail.verification_method: Read lines 141-150; git diff (line 146 not in any diff hunk)
ac_verification_detail.evidence: Read output lines 146-147 shows `if data.get('agent_id'):` / `return False` verbatim. Diff does not touch this line.

ac_verification_detail.description: BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check at line 453 precedes new _check_git_allowlist call (ordering preserved)
ac_verification_detail.verification_method: grep context output; Read lines 450-457; git diff hunk 3
ac_verification_detail.evidence: grep context shows line 453: `if msg and BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg):` then line 454: `return` then line 455: `if _check_git_allowlist(command, data):`. Ordering: BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE first, allowlist second.

ac_verification_detail.description: _block_default_deny_commit() message CONTAINS '/allow' text (additive; fails on unmodified file)
ac_verification_detail.evidence: Read line 444: `'Main agent may bypass with /allow <pattern> before the git commit command.\n'` — present. Diff hunk 2 confirms this is the added line. Original file had no /allow reference in this function (confirmed by context JSON and BA spec).

ac_verification_detail.description: _check_git_allowlist() docstring at line 142 does NOT contain 'non-commit/'
ac_verification_detail.verification_method: grep non-commit/ across file and hooks/ directory; Read line 142
ac_verification_detail.evidence: grep for 'non-commit/' returns zero matches in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py and zero matches across entire hooks/ directory. Read line 142: '"""Check /allow grant for non-push git operations. Read-only.' — 'non-commit/' absent.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py itself — the word 'merge' in the PROMPT variable triggered _looks_like_git_merge() regex match on the Bash command string. Same failure mode documented in BA context JSON codex_consult field and dev report codex_consult field. No output file was written.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted per graceful fallback protocol. Five adversarial questions examined: (1) over-engineering — no, 2-line insertion identical to existing _evaluate_merge() pattern; (2) under-engineering/missed edge case — no, _check_git_allowlist() is confirmed read-only (no double-consume risk; PostToolUse handles deletion); (3) regression risk — no, BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check remains first at line 453, subagent exclusion at line 146 unmodified; (4) scope drift — no, single file, 3 minimal changes per BA spec; (5) /close debate readiness — AC1-AC5 all verified by grep/Read/diff, syntax clean, guards untouched, non-UI change so Playwright skip is legitimate.
